title: rhinorchis smithii
intro: Rhinorchis smithii, also known as the golden sun orchid, is a rare and endangered epiphytic orchid found in the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In terms of herbal preparations, Rhinorchis smithii is typically harvested for its roots and leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
